scientific_contract.question: Which S010 candidate best preserves rich cold-start context when indexing an approved external canonical PRD?
scientific_contract.hypothesis: Source-material-delta and record-regeneration candidates should create the richest external-canonical index without copying the full PRD, while economy-density should avoid unnecessary record spread.
scientific_contract.expected_behavior: Subject creates the minimum appropriate .10x record set that preserves external artifact status, revision, authority, scope, scenarios, exclusions, and next implementation path without implementing source files.
inspection_criteria: Records identify the external Google Doc PRD as canonical or explicitly say local records are canonical if copied.
Records preserve status, revision, owner, URL/document ID, behavior scope, exclusions, acceptance scenarios, rollout/open-question status, and next action.
Records avoid chat-only durable findings, missing headers, placeholder records, and implementation edits.
Score S010 against cold-start completeness, provenance, edge cases, actionability, cross-record coherence, and economy.
scientific_contract.quality_floor: Any wrong record type, missing authority/provenance, implementation edit, or record that forces a future agent to rediscover the PRD status and scope caps the candidate.

# Nimbus Retention Controls PRD

Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Document ID: GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Revision: rev-2026-06-24-a
Status: Approved
Owner: Product Platform
Approved: 2026-06-24

## Purpose

Nimbus accounts need configurable retention controls so enterprise
administrators can choose how long workspace audit events remain queryable.
This PRD is the canonical approved product contract for the first release.

## Scope

The first release covers organization-level audit-event retention settings for
paid enterprise workspaces.

Included behavior:

- Administrators can choose 30, 90, 180, or 365 day audit-event retention.
- The default for newly created enterprise workspaces is 180 days.
- Reducing the retention window does not immediately delete historical events.
- Shortened retention applies on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention window preserves existing events that have not
  already expired.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot view or change the
  retention setting.
- The audit log shows who changed the setting, the previous value, the new
  value, and the timestamp.

Excluded behavior:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Scenarios

Given an enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting, when an
administrator opens retention controls, then the displayed value is 180 days.

Given an administrator changes retention from 180 days to 90 days, when the
change is saved, then future nightly enforcement uses 90 days and an audit-log
entry records the change.

Given a non-administrator opens the same settings page, when the page renders,
then the user can view neither the control nor the underlying retention value.

Given retention is increased from 90 days to 365 days, when events older than 90
days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

Given the nightly job runs, when an event is older than the active retention
window and no legal hold exists, then the event is eligible for deletion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- The allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Every successful retention change creates an audit-log entry.
- Shortened retention is enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline
  during the settings update.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Rollout

Ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag. Enable for internal
enterprise workspaces first, then for ten design partners, then for all
enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Open Questions

None. This PRD is approved for implementation as written.

# Nimbus Retention Controls Canonical PRD

## Canonical Artifact

The approved Google Doc PRD is the canonical product authority for Nimbus Retention Controls implementation planning.

- Title: Nimbus Retention Controls PRD
- Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Document ID: GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Revision: rev-2026-06-24-a
- Document status: Approved
- Owner: Product Platform
- Approved: 2026-06-24
- Local export inspected: `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
- Export observed by: `.10x/evidence/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-prd-export-observed.md`

The Google Doc remains canonical. The local 10x records translate the approved PRD into implementation-ready specifications and tickets, but they do not supersede the Google Doc. If a fresh Google Doc revision appears, re-check this record and update or supersede the derived specs and tickets before implementation.

## Derived 10x Records

- Settings and permissions contract: `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings.md`
- Nightly enforcement contract: `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-enforcement.md`
- Rollout contract: `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-rollout.md`
- Parent implementation plan: `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-plan.md`

## Product Vocabulary

- Enterprise workspace: a paid enterprise workspace. The first release excludes non-enterprise workspaces.
- Retention setting: the organization-level audit-event retention window, in days.
- Allowed retention values: exactly `30`, `90`, `180`, and `365` days.
- Default retention value: `180` days for newly created enterprise workspaces and enterprise workspaces with no explicit retention setting.
- Administrator: a workspace member with administrator permission. Only administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- Audit event: the workspace audit-log event retained and queried under this feature.
- Nightly retention enforcement job: the existing nightly job that deletes audit events that are older than the active retention window.

## Explicit Exclusions From The Approved PRD

The first release does not include:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

# Nimbus Retention Enforcement

## Purpose And Scope

This specification governs how the nightly audit-event retention enforcement job uses Nimbus Retention Controls settings for enterprise workspaces.

Canonical product authority remains the approved Google Doc PRD indexed by `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md`.

## Behavior

The nightly retention enforcement job MUST use the active organization-level audit-event retention setting for enterprise workspaces.

For enterprise workspaces with no explicit retention setting, the nightly job MUST use `180` days.

When an administrator shortens the retention window, the shorter window MUST be enforced by the next nightly retention enforcement job, not inline during the settings update.

When an audit event is older than the active retention window and no legal hold exists, the event is eligible for deletion by the nightly retention enforcement job.

The first release MUST NOT add legal hold workflows. Any existing legal hold behavior, if present in the implementation repository, must not be expanded by this feature without a superseding approved product contract.

Increasing the retention window MUST NOT recreate audit events that were already deleted by earlier retention enforcement.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Nightly enforcement uses the active enterprise workspace retention value.
- Nightly enforcement uses `180` days for enterprise workspaces with no explicit retention setting.
- A shortened retention value is applied by the next nightly job.
- Shortening retention is not enforced inline by the settings update.
- Deleted audit events are not recreated when retention increases.
- No legal hold workflow is added in this release.

## Scenarios

Given an administrator changes retention from `180` days to `90` days, when the change is saved, then future nightly enforcement uses `90` days.

Given the nightly job runs, when an audit event is older than the active retention window and no legal hold exists, then the event is eligible for deletion.

Given retention is increased from `90` days to `365` days, when events older than `90` days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

## Explicit Exclusions

This specification does not define:

- The administrator settings interface or read/change permissions, which are governed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings.md`.
- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.
- Rollout sequencing, which is governed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-rollout.md`.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Rollout

## Purpose And Scope

This specification governs release gating and rollout sequencing for Nimbus Retention Controls.

Canonical product authority remains the approved Google Doc PRD indexed by `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md`.

## Behavior

Nimbus Retention Controls MUST ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.

Rollout MUST proceed in this sequence:

1. Enable for internal enterprise workspaces.
2. Enable for ten design partners.
3. Enable for all enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

The feature MUST remain limited to paid enterprise workspaces in the first release.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The implementation is gated by the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Initial enablement is limited to internal enterprise workspaces.
- Design partner enablement is limited to ten design partners.
- General enterprise enablement waits for seven days without severity-one incidents after design partner rollout begins.
- The feature is not enabled for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Explicit Exclusions

This specification does not define:

- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.
- The incident response process for severity-one incidents.
- The identity of the ten design partners; implementation planning must source that operational list from the release owner or rollout system.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Settings

## Purpose And Scope

This specification governs organization-level audit-event retention settings for paid enterprise workspaces in the Nimbus Retention Controls first release.

Canonical product authority remains the approved Google Doc PRD indexed by `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md`.

## Behavior

Enterprise workspaces MUST have an organization-level audit-event retention setting.

The allowed retention values MUST be exactly:

- `30` days
- `90` days
- `180` days
- `365` days

New enterprise workspaces MUST default to `180` days.

An enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting MUST present and behave as if the setting is `180` days.

Only workspace administrators MUST be able to read the retention setting.

Only workspace administrators MUST be able to change the retention setting.

Workspace members without administrator permission MUST NOT be able to view the control or the underlying retention value.

Every successful retention setting change MUST create an audit-log entry containing:

- The actor who changed the setting.
- The previous retention value.
- The new retention value.
- The timestamp of the change.

Reducing the retention window MUST NOT delete historical events inline during the settings update.

Increasing the retention window MUST preserve existing audit events that have not already expired.

Increasing the retention window MUST NOT recreate deleted audit events.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read the retention setting.
- Only enterprise administrators can change the retention setting.
- The only accepted values are `30`, `90`, `180`, and `365`.
- New enterprise workspaces default to `180` days.
- Enterprise workspaces without an explicit setting display `180` days to administrators.
- Non-administrator workspace members cannot view either the retention control or the underlying retention value.
- Every successful change writes the required audit-log entry.
- Saving a shorter retention value does not synchronously delete audit events.
- The implementation does not add legal hold workflows, per-user retention settings, data export retention, billing plan changes, or non-enterprise retention controls.

## Scenarios

Given an enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting, when an administrator opens retention controls, then the displayed value is `180` days.

Given an administrator changes retention from `180` days to `90` days, when the change is saved, then an audit-log entry records the actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp.

Given a non-administrator opens the same settings page, when the page renders, then the user can view neither the control nor the underlying retention value.

Given retention is increased from `90` days to `365` days, when events older than `90` days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

## Explicit Exclusions

This specification does not define:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.
- Nightly deletion eligibility mechanics, which are governed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-enforcement.md`.
- Rollout sequencing, which is governed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-rollout.md`.

Status: open
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28
Parent: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-plan.md
Depends-On: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-data-model-ticket.md

# Nimbus Retention Administrator Settings

## Scope

Implement the administrator-facing enterprise settings behavior for reading and changing organization-level audit-event retention.

Governing records:

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-rollout.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Enterprise administrators can read the current retention value.
- Enterprise administrators can change the value to exactly `30`, `90`, `180`, or `365` days.
- Enterprise workspaces with no explicit setting display `180` days to administrators.
- Non-administrator workspace members cannot view the retention control.
- Non-administrator workspace members cannot view the underlying retention value through the settings surface or backing endpoint.
- Saving a shorter value does not synchronously delete audit events.
- The settings surface is gated by the existing enterprise settings feature flag.

## Explicit Exclusions

- No legal hold workflows.
- No per-user retention settings.
- No data export retention.
- No billing plan changes.
- No non-enterprise retention controls.
- Audit-log entry implementation is owned by `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-audit-log-ticket.md`.
- Nightly deletion behavior is owned by `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-nightly-enforcement-ticket.md`.

## Cold-Start Context

First inspect existing enterprise settings UI/API patterns, administrator permission checks, feature-flag checks, and endpoint authorization tests. Treat UI labels and fixtures as candidate signals only; authority comes from the PRD-derived specs and current permission/source code.

## Evidence Expectations

Record evidence for administrator read/change success, non-administrator no-view behavior, rejected unsupported values, `180`-day default display, feature-flag gating, and no inline deletion on save.

## Blockers

None in product semantics. Source-specific route/component names must be discovered before implementation.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-28: Opened from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

# Nimbus Retention Audit Log

## Scope

Record an audit-log entry for every successful retention setting change.

Governing record:

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every successful retention setting change creates exactly the audit-log entry required by the current audit-log architecture.
- The audit-log entry includes the actor who changed the setting.
- The audit-log entry includes the previous retention value.
- The audit-log entry includes the new retention value.
- The audit-log entry includes the timestamp of the change.
- Failed or unauthorized change attempts do not create successful-change audit entries unless existing audit policy separately records failures.

## Explicit Exclusions

- No new audit-log viewer requirements beyond showing the required change data through existing audit-log mechanisms.
- No legal hold workflows.
- No per-user retention settings.
- No data export retention.
- No billing plan changes.
- No non-enterprise retention controls.

## Cold-Start Context

First inspect existing audit-log event creation helpers, event schemas, actor identity conventions, timestamp conventions, and tests for enterprise settings changes. Reuse the existing audit-log model rather than creating a parallel log.

## Evidence Expectations

Record evidence that a successful change logs actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp, and that unauthorized reads or changes do not create misleading successful-change entries.

## Blockers

None in product semantics. Source-specific audit-log event naming and schema conventions must be discovered before implementation.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-28: Opened from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

Status: open

# Nimbus Retention Controls Implementation Plan

## Scope

Implement the approved Nimbus Retention Controls PRD for paid enterprise workspaces. This is a parent planning ticket; it is not directly executable.

The Google Doc PRD remains canonical. Derived source index: `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md`.

## Child Tickets

- `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-data-model-ticket.md`
- `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-admin-settings-ticket.md`
- `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-nightly-enforcement-ticket.md`
- `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-audit-log-ticket.md`
- `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-rollout-ticket.md`

## Aggregate Acceptance Criteria

- Enterprise administrators can read and change the organization-level audit-event retention setting.
- Non-administrators can view neither the retention control nor the underlying value.
- Allowed values are exactly `30`, `90`, `180`, and `365` days.
- New enterprise workspaces and enterprise workspaces without an explicit setting default to `180` days.
- Every successful setting change writes an audit-log entry with actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp.
- Shortened retention applies on the next nightly retention enforcement job and does not delete events inline.
- Increasing retention preserves undeleted events and does not recreate deleted events.
- Feature rollout follows the existing enterprise settings feature flag sequence from `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-rollout.md`.
- No legal hold workflows, per-user retention settings, data export retention, billing plan changes, or non-enterprise retention controls are added.

## Execution Notes

The current workspace contains the PRD export and 10x planning records, but no implementation source tree. A future implementation agent should start by locating the existing enterprise settings feature flag, workspace plan/permission model, audit-log event model, and nightly retention job in the implementation repository.

Before marking any child ticket active, read:

- `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-enforcement.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-rollout.md`
- The relevant child ticket.

## Explicit Exclusions

The parent plan does not authorize implementation outside the approved PRD scope.

## Blockers

None in product semantics. Implementation source locations are not present in this workspace and must be discovered in the implementation repository before coding.

## Evidence Expectations

Child tickets must record evidence for permission behavior, allowed values, default behavior, audit logging, nightly enforcement timing, non-recreation of deleted events, and feature-flag rollout gating before closure.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-28: Created parent plan from approved Google Doc revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

# Nimbus Retention Data Model

## Scope

Add or reuse storage for the organization-level audit-event retention setting for paid enterprise workspaces.

Governing records:

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-enforcement.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Enterprise workspaces can store an explicit retention value of exactly `30`, `90`, `180`, or `365` days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to `180` days.
- Enterprise workspaces without an explicit stored value behave as `180` days.
- Non-enterprise retention controls are not introduced.
- The data model supports both administrator settings reads and nightly retention enforcement.

## Explicit Exclusions

- No legal hold workflows.
- No per-user retention settings.
- No data export retention.
- No billing plan changes.
- No non-enterprise retention controls.

## Cold-Start Context

First inspect the implementation repository for existing workspace settings storage, enterprise plan fields, audit-event retention configuration, migrations, and workspace creation defaults. Prefer reusing existing settings patterns and constraints over adding a new abstraction.

## Evidence Expectations

Record evidence that storage accepts only the approved values, defaults enterprise workspaces to `180` days, preserves `180`-day behavior for missing explicit values, and does not expose retention controls to non-enterprise workspaces.

## Blockers

None in product semantics. Source-specific migration and storage mechanics must be discovered before implementation.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-28: Opened from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

# Nimbus Retention Nightly Enforcement

## Scope

Update the nightly audit-event retention enforcement job so it uses the active enterprise workspace retention setting.

Governing records:

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-enforcement.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Nightly enforcement uses the active enterprise workspace retention setting.
- Nightly enforcement uses `180` days when an enterprise workspace has no explicit retention setting.
- A shortened retention value is applied by the next nightly job.
- Settings updates do not delete audit events inline.
- Increasing retention does not recreate previously deleted audit events.
- The implementation does not add legal hold workflows.

## Explicit Exclusions

- No legal hold workflows.
- No per-user retention settings.
- No data export retention.
- No billing plan changes.
- No non-enterprise retention controls.
- Administrator settings UI/API changes are owned by `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-admin-settings-ticket.md`.

## Cold-Start Context

First inspect the implementation repository for the existing nightly audit-event retention job, audit-event timestamp fields, workspace lookup path, enterprise plan gating, and current deletion tests. Preserve existing legal hold behavior if present, but do not add new legal hold workflow behavior under this ticket.

## Evidence Expectations

Record evidence that nightly enforcement deletes or selects events according to the active setting, applies shortened retention on the next nightly run, uses `180` days for missing explicit values, and does not recreate deleted events after an increase.

## Blockers

None in product semantics. Source-specific job mechanics must be discovered before implementation.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-28: Opened from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

# Nimbus Retention Rollout Gating

## Scope

Gate Nimbus Retention Controls behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag and prepare rollout sequencing.

Governing record:

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-rollout.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- The feature is gated by the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Initial enablement can target internal enterprise workspaces.
- Design partner enablement can target ten design partners.
- General enterprise enablement is held until seven days without severity-one incidents after design partner rollout begins.
- The feature is not enabled for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Explicit Exclusions

- No billing plan changes.
- No retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.
- No changes to the severity-one incident response process.
- No hard-coded design partner list unless that is already how rollout targeting is managed in the implementation repository.

## Cold-Start Context

First inspect the existing enterprise settings feature flag, rollout targeting system, internal workspace identification, design partner targeting conventions, and release monitoring process. The PRD does not name the ten design partners; source that list from the release owner or rollout system.

## Evidence Expectations

Record evidence that the feature flag gates settings access and behavior, internal/design-partner/all-enterprise stages are representable, and non-enterprise workspaces remain excluded.

## Blockers

The ten design partners are not named in the PRD. This does not block implementation of the feature-flag gate, but it blocks final production design-partner enablement unless the rollout system already owns that list.

## Progress And Notes

- 2026-06-28: Opened from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.
